objective: Bootstrap worktree squad loops and retrospective-ready process.
whatWentWell: Clear separation of Samwise orchestration and squad-leader execution responsibilities.
Protected-main and isolated-worktree authorization rules were codified early.
whatDidNot: Calibration and loop-log tooling were initially missing and had to be added after qualification review.
openQuestions: How much process overhead will this introduce during very small tickets?
improvements: Run a calibration pass after one full assignment cycle.
Have Rosie mine diary and retrospective artifacts for recurring recommendations.
actionItems: Owner: Samwise - Run first full loop and close with retrospective at next checkpoint.
Owner: Rosie - Run retrospective gardening pass after first real loop closes.

objective: Close the current Taskboard parity checkpoint, capture participant learning, and identify the remaining AJ-required work before any mainline integration.
whatWentWell: Small bounded Taskboard parity slices produced visible improvements while keeping validation green.
Weak UI experiments were reverted instead of being forced into branch history.
The branch stayed clean and the active docs remained aligned with the real state of work.
whatDidNot: NS0 remained artifact-incomplete because a real interactive app session is still required.
Snapshot record mode still crossed a user-only approval boundary during execution.
openQuestions: After the real NS0 interaction run, does Taskboard need one more card-detail parity slice before AJ release review?
How should future checkpoints label artifact completeness more prominently than simple code completeness?
improvements: Separate pair-required work from agent-completable work at the start of each checkpoint.
Keep explicit blocker notes in active docs whenever a required artifact depends on real GUI interaction.
Use the first post-NS0 review to decide whether card-detail hierarchy is the last high-leverage parity gap.
actionItems: Owner: AJ + Samwise - Run the real NS0 interaction session and generate interaction-events.jsonl plus interaction-report.md.
Owner: Studio Interaction Quality Lead - Run a red-pen pass after NS0 closes.
Owner: Venture Product Steward - Produce a readiness memo after NS0 artifacts exist.
Owner: Rosie - Publish prioritized wrap-up recommendations for the next checkpoint.

objective: Garden the Taskboard wrap-up meeting artifacts and convert the checkpoint into evidence-backed recommendations for the next iteration.
whatWentWell: The checkpoint left a strong paper trail across commits, plan docs, partner logs, and retrospective artifacts.
The team explicitly recorded that NS0 is not done until real interaction artifacts exist.
Rejected experiments were treated as useful learning rather than hidden churn.
whatDidNot: Approval interruptions still surfaced during snapshot record mode.
The distinction between code completeness and artifact completeness had to be clarified late in the checkpoint.
openQuestions: Will AJ judge the branch mainline-ready after the NS0 pairing run or request one more detail-polish slice?
Should artifact-completeness become a required field in every future checkpoint closeout?
improvements: Add an artifact-completeness callout to future wrap-up packets.
Classify future checkpoint work as agent-completable versus pair-required before implementation begins.
Treat approval interruptions as first-class retrospective data in the delegated-authority experiment.
actionItems: Owner: AJ + Samwise - Complete the NS0 paired app run and verify generated telemetry artifacts.
Owner: Venture Product Steward - Issue a post-NS0 go/no-go readiness recommendation.
Owner: Samwise - Add artifact-completeness language to the next wrap-up checklist.
